name: Time variation of the fine-structure constant
requirement: Any candidate framework must predict |alpha_dot / alpha| consistent with current atomic-clock-comparison limits. Cross-laboratory comparisons of optical clocks in different atomic species (Yb+ E2 vs E3 octupole, Sr lattice vs Cs fountain, etc.) bound the present-day fractional drift of the fine-structure constant at the ~10^-19 per year level.

regime.description: Atomic-scale physics; precision comparisons of optical-clock transitions sensitive to different powers of alpha.

current_bound.experiment: PTB Yb+ E2/E3 + Cs fountain + (171)Yb lattice clock comparisons
current_bound.reference: Filzinger et al., 'Improved Limits on the Coupling of Ultralight Bosonic Dark Matter to Photons from Optical Atomic Clock Comparisons', PRL 130, 253001 (2023), arXiv:2301.03433

what_it_excludes: Constrains string-theory dilaton scenarios with rolling moduli, varying-constant cosmologies (Bekenstein-Sandvik-Barrow-Magueijo), and ultralight scalar dark-matter models with photon couplings. Combined with redshifted-quasar-absorption constraints (which probe alpha at z ~ 1-3 to ~10^-6 fractional precision), this benchmark restricts the entire late-time history of fundamental constants.

parameterization.name: Direct dimensionless: d(ln alpha)/dt. For ultralight scalar dark matter coupled to photons, parameterized by coupling d_e in the dilaton-charge convention.
parameterization.summary: A scalar field phi with Lagrangian -(1/4) (1 + d_e phi/M_Pl) F_munu F^munu shifts alpha proportionally to phi. Time-evolution of phi (e.g. cosmological rolling, oscillating dark matter) sources alpha-dot.

procedural.predicted_observable: alpha_dot / alpha = (d_e / M_Pl) * phi_dot for a slowly-rolling scalar; for oscillating ULDM phi(t) = phi_0 cos(m_phi t), the comparison is between clock-comparison Allan variance at frequency m_phi and the predicted oscillation amplitude.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: |alpha_dot / alpha| < 2.1 x 10^-18 / yr (95% CL). For ULDM photon coupling: |d_e| < ~10^-5 at m_phi ~ 10^-22 eV (improving rapidly).
framework_inputs: If the framework predicts a varying alpha (cosmological evolution, environmental dependence, or oscillation), compute its time derivative at the present epoch in laboratory frame.
If the framework includes ultralight scalars coupled to photons, compute the dilaton-photon coupling d_e and the scalar mass m_phi.
procedural.notes: Atomic clocks also bound mu_dot/mu (proton-electron mass ratio) via molecular vibrational/rotational transitions. A serious framework predicting varying constants must respect both.

primary_references.citation: Filzinger et al., 'Improved Limits on the Coupling of Ultralight Bosonic Dark Matter to Photons from Optical Atomic Clock Comparisons', PRL 130, 253001 (2023)
Lange et al., 'Improved Limits for Violations of Local Position Invariance from Atomic Clock Comparisons', PRL 126, 011102 (2021)
Webb et al., 'Indications of a Spatial Variation of the Fine Structure Constant', PRL 107, 191101 (2011)
primary_references.note: Quasar-absorption constraints; sensitivity to spatial variation.
